name: Oriental Chicken Topping
recipeIngredient: 2 small boneless skinless chicken breast halves
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, heat oil. Add chicken and cook, stirring until browned and cooked through, about 6 to 7 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, remove the chicken and set aside. Add onion, red pepper, and carrots; cook until crisp-tender, about 4 to 5 minutes. Stir in snow peas, chicken broth, and ginger. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat. Simmer until vegetables are tender, about 4 to 5 minutes.
2. Return the cooked chicken to the skillet. In a small bowl combine cornstarch with soy sauce. Add this mixture to the skillet; stir. Bring to a boil and cook until thickened, about 1 minute.
3. Cut each potato in half lengthwise, as close to the base as possible. Mash slightly with a fork, leaving in skins. Spoon the chicken and vegetable mixture over each potato, dividing evenly.
Makes 4 servings. Preparation time: About 30 minutes. Cooking time: About 15 minutes. Per serving: Approximately 267 calories, 17 grams protein, 34 grams carbohydrates, 5 grams fat, 17% of calories from fat, 35 milligrams cholesterol, and 450 milligrams sodium.
